text.title: Tech Radar (ThoughtWorks-style) Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Tech Radar (ThoughtWorks-style) application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Quadrants: Adopt (use it), Trial (try it), Assess (watch it), Hold (avoid it). Don't invent new states.
rule.rationale: ThoughtWorks 4-quadrant is the lingua franca; new states create confusion + miss the network effect.

rule.statement: Update quarterly. Stale radar (>6 months) = ignored radar.
rule.rationale: Radar updated annually loses relevance; quarterly is the sustainable cadence.

rule.statement: Each blip cites ≥2 internal projects using (or considering) it. Pure-opinion blips drift.
rule.rationale: Evidence-backed blips are reviewed; opinion blips are litigated.

rule.statement: Maintainers list ≥2 people with authority to update. Single-maintainer radar dies on departure.
rule.rationale: Single point of failure on documentation = guaranteed death.

rule.statement: Blip history (when added, moved, deprecated) preserved. 'Why we moved Y from Trial to Hold' is the value.
rule.rationale: Without history, lessons evaporate; history compounds team learning.
